steps.description: Clone the upstream SongFormer repository into the model-local directory and record the tested commit for evidence and repro manifests.
Create an isolated model-local Python 3.10 environment without mutating the global conda installation.

steps.command: .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel && .venv/bin/pip install torch==2.4.0 torchaudio==2.4.0 transformers==4.51.1 huggingface-hub==0.30.1 accelerate==1.5.2 safetensors==0.5.3 numpy==1.25.0 scipy==1.15.2 librosa==0.11.0 soundfile==0.13.1 omegaconf==2.3.0 ema-pytorch==0.7.7 x-transformers==2.4.14 msaf==0.1.80 muq==0.1.0 loguru==0.7.3
steps.description: Install the smallest dependency set supported by upstream README and repo-native imports for the Hugging Face AutoModel path; avoid training-only extras unless runtime evidence later proves they are required.

steps.command: HF_HOME=hf_cache .venv/bin/python -c "from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download; snapshot_download(repo_id='ASLP-lab/SongFormer', repo_type='model', local_dir='ckpts/SongFormer', local_dir_use_symlinks=False, resume_download=True, allow_patterns='*', ignore_patterns=['SongFormer.pt', 'SongFormer.safetensors'])"
steps.description: Download the Hugging Face model snapshot into the model-local ckpts directory and direct secondary Hugging Face cache writes into a model-local HF_HOME.
Run import/load/infer/contract against the verified 24kHz music fixture and persist structured validation artifacts.

notes: The selected fixture is a model-specific fallback generated from an existing shared MIR fixture because the prompt path does not exist in-repo.
ffmpeg is absent on host, but BUILD_ENV does not require it because the verified fallback fixture is already a compliant 24kHz mono PCM WAV asset.
GPU is unavailable on this host, so any successful phase-1 run must use the same AutoModel repo-native path on CPU and record the limitation explicitly.
